zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Le Strange於1308年出生在英國史洛普爾其其時，她的父親Fulk Strange42歲，母親Eleanor Giffard34歲。她於大約1329年與Robert Corbet在英國英格蘭史洛普爾沙波利結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1381年7月1日在英國史洛普爾莫雷頓 可特享年73歲，埋葬在英國史洛普爾阿爾波波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Le Strange于1308年出生在英国史洛普爾其其时，她的父亲Fulk Strange42岁，母亲Eleanor Giffard34岁。她于大约1329年与Robert Corbet在英国英格兰史洛普爾沙波利结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1381年7月1日在英国史洛普爾莫雷頓 可特享年73岁，埋葬在英国史洛普爾阿爾波波利。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Strange
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Wiltshire and Gloucestershire): nickname for a newcomer to a district, from Middle English strange ‘foreign’ (Old French estrange, Latin extraneus, from extra ‘outside’).

Irish (Antrim): this name is also common in Ireland, where, in addition to its English etymology, it may represent an Anglicized form of any of several Irish names containing the element gall ‘stranger’, e.g. Doyle , Gallagher .

variants.name: Stranger
meanings: English: nickname for a newcomer to an area, from Middle English stra(u)nger(e) ‘stranger, foreigner’ (Old French estrangier). Compare Strange .

variants.name: Doyle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Sean, Kieran, Seamus, Aileen, Dermot, Liam, Niall, Brennan, Brigid, Conal, Conan, Donal.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Dubhghaill ‘descendant of Dubhghall’, a personal name composed of the elements dubh ‘black’ + gall ‘stranger’. This was used as a byname for Scandinavians, in particular to distinguish the darker-haired Danes from fair-haired Norwegians. Compare McDougall , McDowell .

variants.name: Lestrange
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Strange 1 with fused Old French definite article le.

Irish: in Ireland, adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche ‘son of Cúchoigcriche’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the borderland’. Lestrange was adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche probably due to the presence of a prominent family of Lestrange, of similar meaning, who settled in the counties of Westmeath and Offaly in the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Le Strange was born in 1308, in Whitchurch, Shropshire, England, her father, Fulk Strange, was 42 and her mother, Eleanor Giffard, was 34. She married Robert Corbet about 1329, in Shawbury, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 7 daughters. She died on 1 July 1381, in Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, England, at the age of 73, and was buried in Alberbury, Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Le Strange est née en 1308, à Whitchurch, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Fulk Strange, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Eleanor Giffard, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé Robert Corbet environ 1329, à Shawbury, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 juillet 1381, à Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 73 ans, et a été enterrée à Alberbury, Shropshire, Angleterre.

Juli 1381 im Alter von 73 Jahren in Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, England und wurde in Alberbury, Shropshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Le Strange è nata il nel 1308 nel Whitchurch, Shropshire, England, sua padre, Fulk Strange, aveva 42 anni e sua madre, Eleanor Giffard, aveva 34 anni. Lei sposato Robert Corbet circa 1329, nel Shawbury, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 1 luglio 1381, nel Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, England, all'età di 73 anni, e fu sepolta a Alberbury, Shropshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Le Strangeが1308年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアウィトチャーチで生まれた時，父Fulk Strangeは42歳，母Eleanor Giffardは34歳であった。約1329年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアシャウブリーでRobert Corbetと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1381年7月1日にイギリスシュロプシュアイアモーエトン コルベットで73歳で死去し，イギリスシュロプシュアイアアルバーブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Le Strange(이)가 1308년 영국 슈럽실르 휘트c후르치에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Fulk Strange(은)는 42세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eleanor Giffard(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 대략 1329년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 쇼버이에서 Robert Corbet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1381년 7월 1일 영국 슈럽실르 모레턴 코벳에서 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 슈럽실르 알버버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Le Strange nasceu em 1308, em Whitchurch, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Fulk Strange, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor Giffard, tinha 34 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert Corbet aproximadamente 1329, em Shawbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1 de julho de 1381, em Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 73 anos, e foi sepultada em Alberbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Le Strange родилась в 1308 г. в местности уичурч, шропшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Fulk Strange было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Eleanor Giffard, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Corbet около 1329 г. в местности шавбури, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Она умерла 1 июля 1381 г. в местности моретон корбет, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 73 лет и была похоронена в местности альбирбури, шропшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Le Strange nació en 1308, en Whitchurch, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Fulk Strange, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Eleanor Giffard, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Robert Corbet aproximadamente 1329, en Shawbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 1 de julio de 1381, en Moreton Corbet, Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 73 años, y fue sepultada en Alberbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra.
